From: Jcs [Personal
]
Sent: Fri 07/02/2025 5:49:48 PM (UTC)
To: Jcs [Personal ]
Subject: FW: Peter Mandelson STRAP Form

Grrrr, sorry about this — but could you ask Peter to share with us the final list of foreign
contacts he named in his DV form? We can populate for him, but sadly we didn’ t see the final
list he sent back to UKSV. And it sounds like the two need to align. He will grumble about this,
for which apologies..

JCS —_| Head of US Global Issues and Canada Team | US and Canada
Department | Americas and SIDS Directorate | Foreign, Commonwealth &
Development Office | E: Personal | M: Personal

ail form please.

From:

Peter Mandelson
Subject:
RE: 20250115 - 
 - Additional Document Request - OSP
Ha
I suggest you send over the handful of names you mentioned, even though you don’t consider 
them “close contacts”. That will reassure the vetting team that you’ve been comprehensive, 
even if it’s all quite artificial
 | Head of US Global Issues and Canada Team | US and Canada 
Department | Americas and SIDS Directorate | Foreign, Commonwealth & 
Development Office |
From: Peter Mandelson 
Sent: 22 January 2025 17:36
To: 
 
Subject: Fwd: 20250115 - 
 - Additional Document Request - OSP
What is 
 saying ?
Sent from my iPhone
Begin forwarded message:
From: 
Date: 22 January 2025 at 14:54:29 GMT
To: Peter Mandelson 
Subject: Re: 20250115 - 
 - Additional Document Request - 
OSP
Good afternoon,
Thank you for your enquiry, I have referred this back to the management 
of the Special Handling team for clarification. It is prudent that we obtain 
sufficient information about overseas contacts that we can be confident 
that any potential security risks are identified. Therefore, it would be better 
to have more information than we need rather than less so that UKSV and 
FCDO can assess each individual on their own merit.
Kind regards,
EDV Case Assistant
UKSV, Government Security Group
Cabinet Office
E:
NSV Privacy Notice
parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
